key_insight: Adding a controlled amount of Al to AT40 plasma-sprayed coatings densifies the microstructure and boosts toughness without sacrificing hardness.
engineering_relevance: The work shows how metal–ceramic hybrid design and spray-parameter tuning can produce more durable protective coatings for wear- and impact-critical components.
method: Prepared AT40/Al composite coatings by atmospheric plasma spraying with Al contents of 10%, 20%, and 30%.
Varied key spray parameters: arc power, spraying distance, and gun traverse speed.
Used SEM, XRD, EDS, and grey relational analysis to link microstructure to performance.
evidence_signal: The best coating condition was 38 kW, 100 mm, and 400 mm s−1, giving 519.68 HV hardness and a 45.3% impact-resistance improvement, while 10% Al reduced porosity to 3.2% from 8.5% for pure AT40.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on tight control of Al fraction and spray conditions, since excessive deviation can reduce densification and interfacial quality.
paper_title: Preparation and Mechanism Study of Plasma-Sprayed Metal–Ceramic Composite Coatings Based on Microstructure
